cards.title: Premium Calculation
cards.content:
ΓÇó Mortality (Death Rate): Higher mortality = Higher premium.
ΓÇó Interest: Higher interest earned by insurer = Lower premium.
ΓÇó Expenses (Loading): Adds to the premium cost.

cards.title: Actuarial Valuation
cards.content:
ΓÇó The process by which an Actuary assesses the assets and future liabilities of an insurance company.
ΓÇó If Assets > Liabilities, the surplus is distributed as Bonus to participating policyholders.

items.text: Net Premium = Mortality Cost - Interest Earned. (Does NOT include expenses).
Gross Premium = Net Premium + Expenses (Loading) + Contingency Margins.
You pay the same flat premium amount every year, even though mortality risk increases with age.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Mortality Table: A statistical table showing the probability of death at various ages (e.g., IALM Table).
Actuary: The highly specialized mathematician who calculates premiums, reserves, and bonuses.
Reversionary Bonus: Declared annually but paid only at maturity or death.
Terminal Bonus: A one-time extra bonus paid only when the policy matures or the person dies.
Policy Reserve: The extra money collected in early years (under level premium) is kept aside to pay future claims.
